attestation.statement: Clinician confirmed in writing having clinically reviewed all 49 CVD (Cardiovascular) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)

transform_flags: case_id SEQ provisional (from CDV-044) - maintainer to confirm/reassign within SPEC-CARD-03 bucket

difficulty_tier red_herring_laden (03): fine tremor tempts a thyrotoxicosis anchor and atypical chest heaviness plus anxiety tempts an ACS/panic anchor, while the true answer is a benign reversible sinus tachycardia - tier driven by the discrimination difficulty in a haemodynamically stable presentation with a clear reversible driver
diagnosis_category common - sinus tachycardia from a reversible cause is a common presentation
age 45 stated (adult); sex not stated in source - inferred female for narrative consistency only, flagged for clinician confirmation
next of kin not specified (lives alone) - contact_nok relationship 'not_specified', available false

telehealth reprojection applied - in-person home assessment (clinician vitals HR 128/BP 125/80/Temp 36.8/RR 18/SpO2 98%, cardiovascular/respiratory/neuro exam, fine resting tremor, diaphoresis, bounding pulses, no goitre) reprojected: sealed into 10/11 + telehealth_limits; patient-reportable palpitations/self-counted pulse (~120-130, regular) and reported shakiness/anxiety carried to 01, tagged patient_reported/unverified
12-lead ECG to confirm sinus rhythm and TSH/routine bloods are clinician/investigation-only - sealed to 10/11 as investigation_result; source anticipated sinus tachycardia vs SVT on formal tracing

no antiarrhythmic/rate-control medication indicated (not_indicated_here); caffeine/energy drinks encoded should_NOT_recommend as a reversible trigger to eliminate; slow controlled-breathing/vagal manoeuvre included as a telehealth-coachable measure; no dose fabricated

baseline tier T3 (same-day in-person ECG confirmation of sinus rhythm) is conservative for a stable sustained unexplained tachycardia that cannot be rhythm-confirmed remotely; source did urgent after-hours in-person assessment then arranged routine GP ECG/bloods - reprojected as same-day confirmation with routine TSH follow-up; ESC to T5 (000) if instability/ischaemic features develop; over-triage to ambulance/ED flagged as unnecessary for this stable reversible presentation

no PMH/allergies/FHx/guideline citation in source - left empty/omitted; comorbidities empty; reversible causes checklist (fever, dehydration, anaemia, thyrotoxicosis, anxiety, haemorrhage) modelled in pathophysiology_summary and differentials

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
